word: G2245 - ἡλίκος - hēlíkos - hay-lee'-kos - How great

sections.paragraph:
- Original: ἡλίκος

- Transliteration: helikos

- Phonetic: hay-lee'-kos

- Definition:

1. as old as, as tall as

a. how great

b. how small

- Origin: from helix (a comrade, i.e. one of the same age)

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech: Adjective

- Strong's: From ἧλιξ hē lix (a comrade that is one of the same age); as big as that is (interjectively) how much: - how (what) great.
